title: Add play/pause animation to forecast timeline
description: Adds a Play button to the forecast timeline that auto-advances through hourly forecast data, animating the weather particles, conditions panel, and time display in real time. This lets users visualize how conditions evolve over the forecast window without manually dragging the slider.

plan.summary:
Add a play/pause toggle to the existing forecast timeline panel. When playing, the app auto-advances through each hourly forecast step at a configurable interval, updating the conditions panel, particle animations, and timeline display on each tick. The slider thumb moves along with playback, and clicking pause (or reaching the end) stops it. This is a client-side-only change — no new API endpoints or backend logic needed, since all hourly forecast data is already fetched and stored in `currentWeather.hourly[]`.

The implementation touches three existing entities: the template (add a play button to the timeline header), the CSS (style the button and its playing state), and the JS (add play/pause state machine with interval-based advancement). A new config `TIMELINE_PLAY_INTERVAL_MS` sets the default speed.

Tradeoff: the compass rose (Direction Analysis) won't animate during playback since it's based on a single API snapshot of current conditions, not per-hour data. This is acceptable — the compass shows the "right now" picture, while playback visualizes the forecast timeline.

steps.description: Add a TIMELINE_PLAY_INTERVAL_MS config (default 1500ms) to control playback speed — referenced in the JS for the auto-advance interval.
Update the sailing-app template: add a play/pause button inside the .timeline-header div, between the label and the time display. The button uses ▶ / ❚❚ text content to indicate state.

Update the sailing-weather.css: style the play button (#timeline-play-btn) to match the existing dark nautical theme — small circular button with accent color, hover/active states, and a .playing class that swaps the icon appearance. Also add a subtle pulse animation on the slider thumb while playing.

Update the sailing-weather.js: add play/pause state management (isPlaying boolean, playInterval timer ID), wire up the play button click handler to toggle playback, implement the advanceTimeline() function that increments currentHourIndex each tick (updating conditions panel, particles, slider position, and time display), and auto-stop + reset to beginning when reaching the end of the forecast. Pause when the user manually drags the slider during playback. Fetch fresh config for interval speed.
Add tests: verify the play button exists in the template, verify the config is readable, and run an interactive browser test clicking the play button and verifying the timeline time display changes.
